mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in >65% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer discretionary and fixed investment channels, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20d returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding its 12-month moving average by more than 2 standard deviations signals supply-side overheating and impending capacity constraints, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings above the 2-sigma threshold are followed by positive 5d returns in >70% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power during monetary easing phases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding corporate revenue bases and wage growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions consistently coincide with negative 20d forward returns, yielding a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 0.4 percent signal accelerating price pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% MoM are followed by positive 60d returns in >60% of instances, or the signal fails to predict returns when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive GDP growth and rising unemployment indicates jobless expansion where corporate profits decouple from household income, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising GDPC1 and rising UNRATE shows no statistical association with 60d forward returns, or positive returns dominate in >55% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines during periods of ultra-low policy rates signal structural supply weaknesses that cannot be offset by monetary accommodation, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during low-rate environments are followed by positive 5d returns in >65% of cases, or the predictive coefficient flips sign when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%.
